app.name: ai-rulez
app.one_liner: Directory-based AI governance for 18+ tools that generates synchronized, native configurations from a single source of truth.

screens.name: AI Governance Dashboard
screens.purpose: Central interface to manage unified AI rules, skills, agents, and commands across all supported tools.
primary_actions: Configure profiles for team-specific rule sets
Generate configs with npx ai-rulez generate
Initialize project with npx ai-rulez init

rust_commands.name: apply_profile
rust_commands.purpose: Select and merge domains according to a named profile (e.g., backend, frontend) during config generation.

rust_commands.name: generate_configs
rust_commands.purpose: Generate native configuration files for all enabled AI tools based on .ai-rulez/ definitions, applying profile and include resolution.

rust_commands.name: resolve_includes
rust_commands.purpose: Fetch and integrate remote rule sets from git repositories into the local domain resolution pipeline.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add profile resolution logic to select domain subsets during generation
week 1: Build generator registry supporting at least Claude and Cursor presets with correct frontmatter placement
week 1: Implement core directory parser for .ai-rulez/rules/, .ai-rulez/skills/, .ai-rulez/agents/, .ai-rulez/commands/
week 1: Integrate remote include resolver with fail-fast validation
acceptance_tests: Given a .github/copilot-instructions.md output, then .gitignore contains only the file path—not the entire .github/ directory.
Given a Claude skill definition, when generating SKILL.md, then YAML frontmatter appears before any HTML comment header.
Given a profile 'backend' with domains [backend, database], when generating, then only rules/skills from those domains are included.
Given an include referencing a remote repo, when generating configs, then included domains are resolved and merged before profile-specific domains (fix for E-IS-001).
